body.title: [sprint] Define PROOF METHOD (names a thing) and LEVER (asserts a property) — they do different jobs; neither is abolished. Fix the causal sentence in CLAUDE.md.
body.intention: I intend that 'proof method' and 'lever' are both DEFINED and each used for its own job: proof method NAMES the mechanism that makes a CLASS of claim provable; lever ASSERTS the property that a thing is small relative to what it keeps unlocking. And that the causal sentence is corrected everywhere it appears: the PROOF CORPUS earns a gate's removal — never the backlog, and never a lever merely NAMED.
body.description:
**⚠ PREMISE REVERSED 2026-07-13 — RE-SCOPED FROM 'ABOLISH LEVER'.** This ticket was filed on the theory that 'lever' is a bad word (a force metaphor that smuggles in a causal step) and should be renamed away. Akien ruled the opposite on 2026-07-13: the word is real, important, and now has a formal definition (devlab/runtime/memory/rules/ -> lever-definition).

**A LEVER IS A SMALL THING THAT KEEPS DOING BIG WORK** — ratio, not force, not scale. And it is a PROPERTY, NOT A CATEGORY: any artifact can carry it. Akien: 'i don't think levers and proofs are related to one another individually. But proofs can certainly be levers. The 89% proof of our model at Delta will be a lever there. a big one. perhaps in the industry at large.'

**THE TWO WORDS DO DIFFERENT JOBS.** 'Proof method' NAMES A THING (the mechanism that makes a CLASS provable — a noun with a referent). 'Lever' ASSERTS A PROPERTY (small relative to what it keeps paying — a claim, which can be wrong, and must be EARNED). Not synonyms; not substitutable; neither abolished.

**THE HONEST DIAGNOSIS OF THE ORIGINAL ERROR.** It was NEVER the wrong word — and it was not a genus/species confusion either (that was CC's own over-modelling on 2026-07-13, cut by Akien the same day: lever is a predicate, not a category). THE ERROR WAS ASSERTING THE PROPERTY WITHOUT EARNING IT — treating a NAMED lever as one that had already PAID. A lever pays only when BUILT AND USED; naming one is a DEBT, not a credit. Renaming fixes nothing. **The build work below is therefore about DEFINITION and the CAUSAL SENTENCE, not about a rename.**

**ALSO GUARD THE CP4 BOUNDARY (Akien 2026-07-13):** not everything that reduces friction is a lever. CP4 is the value (reduce friction, unconditional); the lever is the ECONOMICS of CP4 (where the reduction is cheapest per unit). If everything is a lever the word stops sorting, and 'find the lever' — a real instruction — degrades into 'make things nicer'. The word earns its keep by being SCARCE.

**RE-SCOPED WORK:** (1) the rules/ artifact defining proof / proof-method / proof-corpus AND lever (lever-definition is EMITTED 2026-07-13 — remaining: the proof-side terms). (2) Fix CLAUDE.md's sentence ('the accumulated missing-levers are the backlog that earns the gates' eventual removal' — FALSE; the corpus earns it). (3) The `missing_lever` -> `missing_proof_method` identifier rename in code STILL STANDS, but for a different reason than originally given: the FIELD names a thing (the mechanism we lack), so it should use the naming word, not the asserting word. Back-compat read of the old key as before.

**CARRIER FOUND 2026-07-13:** T-unproven-lever-hygiene's `intention:` field carried the retracted claim verbatim ('a queryable backlog that earns gate removal'). Corrected in place. This ticket's sweep should confirm no OTHER live ticket intention repeats it. Note Akien's rule of the same day: the intention field is the FIRST thing a builder reads, so a stale one costs MORE than an absent one.

--- ORIGINAL TICKET TEXT BELOW (premise superseded; the measurements and the causal chain are still correct and still the reference) ---

Akien, 2026-07-09: 'what means levers here?' then 'maybe we just need a different term to make it easier for you to catch it?'

**THE TERM CAUSED A REAL ERROR, MEASURED.** CC wrote the contract line 'The gates are scaffolding; the missing-levers backlog earns their removal.' That is FALSE. It welds CLAUDE.md's sentence ('the accumulated missing-levers are the backlog that earns the gates' eventual removal') onto Akien's stricter statement of 2026-06-21 ('the PROOF CORPUS is what earns the right to remove a gate... Removing a gate without accumulated proof is just hoping — it reintroduces the hollow build we're curing (CP1). No gate comes off on optimism.'), skipping the two middle steps.

The real chain: name the missing proof method -> build it -> the CLASS becomes provable -> proofs accumulate -> the proof corpus earns the gate's removal. The backlog is the MAP of what is unprovable. The corpus is the PAYMENT.

**WHY THE WORD DID IT.** 'Lever' is a metaphor of force — levers move things — so the sentence reads as causally complete and the metaphor silently supplies the missing step. An inert noun cannot. 'Proof method' is standard, unglamorous, and drags the missing word CLASS back into every sentence: you cannot write 'the missing-proof-methods backlog earns gate removal' without hearing that a method earns nothing.

**THREE THINGS, TWO WORDS.** proof = one instance. lever = the mechanism that makes a class provable. proof corpus = the accumulation that earns a gate's removal. The middle one is the only one with a bad name, and the third has NO name. That is why the second collapsed into the third.

**IT IS DEFINED NOWHERE.** grep: 'lever' appears in no rules/ artifact. Its only definition anywhere is a subordinate clause in CLAUDE.md line 138 — 'the lever that makes the class provable'. Undefined jargon in the one document that arbitrates conflicts is how it drifted.

**CARRIER FOUND 2026-07-13 (convergence check on the intention corpus):** T-unproven-lever-hygiene's `intention:` field carried the retracted claim verbatim ("a queryable backlog that earns gate removal"). Its intention field is corrected in place; listing it here so this ticket's sweep confirms no OTHER live ticket intention repeats it. Note the failure mode Akien named 2026-07-13: the intention field is the FIRST thing a builder reads, so a stale one costs more than an absent one.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/_builder_close.py (missing_lever param + docstring), unseen_university/ticket_status.py (comment), 15 code sites total by grep; CLAUDE.md (6 lines); skills/ (4 files); a NEW rules/ artifact defining proof-method / proof / proof-corpus.
**Design rules:** annotate-never-delete (CP2) — the 227 historical memory-store files mentioning 'lever' are HISTORY and are NOT rewritten; canonical memory home devlab/runtime/memory/; rules artifacts carry statement + why (see T-rules-store-materialize).
**Scope boundary:** IN — rename the code identifier missing_lever -> missing_proof_method with a back-compat read of the old key; update CLAUDE.md and the 4 skills; emit a rules/ artifact defining all THREE terms and their causal chain. OUT — rewriting the 227 historical store records; renaming 'red form' (that is the shape of ONE proof and must stay distinct); building any proof method (T-mutation-red-retroactive-proof is the first one).
**Test plan:** (a) grep proves zero remaining `missing_lever` identifiers in code, and that reading a ticket closed with the OLD key still resolves (back-compat). (b) A rules_citation_check-style test asserts the proof-method rule artifact exists and resolves. (c) The discriminating test: assert the rules artifact states that the PROOF CORPUS earns gate removal and the BACKLOG does not — a presence-check on the word 'proof method' would pass on template-dressed vagueness, so the node must assert the causal claim. Red form: no rules artifact exists; the term is defined nowhere.
**Completion criteria:** `grep -rn missing_lever --include=*.py` returns 0; a ticket closed with the old key still reads back; rules/ carries a proof-method artifact defining proof vs proof-method vs proof-corpus and stating that the corpus, not the backlog, earns gate removal.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.completion_criteria: A rules/ artifact defines proof / proof-method / proof-corpus and states that the CORPUS earns gate removal, not the backlog and not a named-but-unbuilt lever. rules/lever-definition (already emitted) is cross-linked. CLAUDE.md's 'backlog earns the gates' removal' sentence is corrected. `grep -rn missing_lever --include=*.py` returns 0, and a ticket closed with the OLD key still reads back (back-compat). No live ticket `intention:` field asserts that the backlog earns gate removal. No historical store record is rewritten (CP2 — annotate, never delete).
